title: Gate Maintenance, Upgrades, Metrics, and Capstone · Policy Workshop
summary: Operate a reusable repository template without letting gates rot or overwhelm contributors. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a reusable quality-gated repository template while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure defect escape, false positives, duration, flakiness, suppression age, and developer friction.
Schedule compatibility-tested tool and runtime upgrades with rollback paths.
Raise standards gradually with changed-code gates and expiring debt records.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-capstone-defense-1bczny: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Capstone Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-capstone-defense-1bczny:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

stimulus.title: Capstone Defense failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A fashionable tool list is a quality strategy.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend each gate by risk, ownership, feedback time, evidence, and maintenance plan.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Capstone Defense condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Capstone Defense applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend each gate by risk, ownership, feedback time, evidence, and maintenance plan.
